title: Gold Falls as Traders Weigh Fed Rate Path and Hormuz Attacks
summary: Gold extended a pullback from a two-week high, as traders weighed the outlook for US interest rates and renewed attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Mag 7 Loses Market Swagger as AI Trade Spreads Beyond Behemoths
summary: For years, the Magnificent Seven tech giants commanded investors’ attention, dominating the S&P 500 Index and determining which way the overall stock market was headed. Those days are over.

title: US Stocks Fall as Samsung Hits Global Chipmakers: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell as volatility hit chipmakers anew after blowout earnings from Samsung Electronics Co. left investors wanting even more. Bonds slipped as Brent crude rose by the most in more than a week.

title: Wall Street Buys Into Musk’s SpaceX Vision
summary: SpaceX is joining the Nasdaq 100 Index and analysts at big global brokers are recommending investors buy shares.

title: France Cuts 2026 GDP Forecast Due to Budget Delay, Iran Conflict
summary: The French government is lowering its growth forecast for the year to 0.7% after a delayed budget and the Middle East conflict held back output, Finance Minister Roland Lescure told Le Parisien newspaper.

title: AI Productivity Gains Are Years Away, Deutsche Bank’s Reid Says
summary: Artificial intelligence is likely to enhance productivity signficantly but any such impact on economies may still be years away, Deutsche Bank analyst Jim Reid said.

title: A Once-Great Copper Miner Faces a Reckoning Just as AI Power Demand Soars
summary: Chile’s Codelco, facing scrutiny for a fatal accident and inflated production figures, is struggling under $25 billion of debt.

title: Chile revisa el futuro de Codelco en medio del boom mundial de la demanda de cobre
summary: A medida que se dispara la demanda mundial de cobre, una de las mayores empresas mineras del mundo se encuentra en una situación precaria.

title: Barclays Sees Forint Rally Over as Tough Road to Euro Starts
summary: The forint rally triggered by Hungary’s political shift is fading as Prime Minister Peter Magyar faces a daunting task to prepare the country for euro accession, according to a strategist at Barclays Plc.

title: China Regulator Is Keeping a Close Eye on Susquehanna Case
summary: China’s securities watchdog said it is paying close attention to a legal case brought by Susquehanna International Group against alleged insider traders, after the US market maker said regulatory officials may have been involved in the trades.

title: How Michaels Became an Unlikely Success
summary: Arts and crafts store Michaels has gone from a "grandmother’s store" to an unlikely business success. Michelle Cheng breaks down how. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Climbs as Fresh Tanker Strike Highlights Risks Around Hormuz
summary: Oil nudged higher following attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting continued risks to vessels in the critical waterway.

title: Goldman Says Earnings to Drive More Gains in Asset-Heavy Stocks
summary: Capital-intensive companies are likely to report solid earnings this season, further outperforming peers that are more reliant on human or digital assets, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strategists.

title: Czech Policymaker Cautions on Services Prices as Inflation Slows
summary: A Czech central banker signaled that tight monetary policy remains appropriate even after headline inflation slowed more than expected last month in the wake of the first interest-rate hike in four years.

title: LNG Tanker Attack Threatens Qatar’s Efforts to Revive Hormuz Exports
summary: A projectile hit the Al Rekayyat gas carrier on Tuesday, with the repercussions reverberating through the global gas market.

title: Extreme SK Hynix Stock Swings Add Wild Card to $28 Billion Deal
summary: SK Hynix Inc.’s New York listing, currently on track to be the largest ever by a foreign company in the US, is facing an unusual source of uncertainty for a major offering: the wildest swings seen in global chip stocks in years.

title: Uzbekistan’s AKFA Picks Citi, JPM to Sound Out Bond Investors
summary: AKFA Aluminium Group, one of Uzbekistan’s largest private companies, is set to meet international investors next week as it explores issuing its first ever bond.

title: Hong Kong Begins Trial Operation of New Gold Clearing System
summary: Hong Kong has launched the trial operation of a new gold clearing system, backed by several major banks, in a key step toward its ambition to become a significant bullion-trading hub with price-setting power.

title: Samsung’s Record Profit Fails to Impress After AI Chip Rally
summary: Samsung Electronics Co.’s quarterly profit surged 19-fold, prompting investors to cash out of a near-150% rally this year that had baked in the AI-fueled growth.

title: China’s PBOC Buys Most Gold Since 2023 as Bullion Swings
summary: China’s central bank bought more gold in June, extending the longest buying streak since at least 2015 and underscoring a commitment to diversifying its reserves despite volatility in bullion prices.

title: Italy Revives Dollar Bond Sales for First Time Since Pandemic
summary: Italy is returning to the US dollar bond market for the first time since the pandemic, broadening its pool of foreign investors after the country’s improving finances won credit-rating upgrades.

title: Foreign Funds Return to India’s Banks After $12 Billion Selloff
summary: Global investors returned to India’s financial stocks after a three-month selling streak, as the central bank’s recent measures to draw foreign capital ease funding constraints for lenders.

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 7/7/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: PBOC Unveils Measures to Cement Hong Kong’s Yuan Hub Status
summary: China’s central bank announced a slew of measures aimed at enhancing Hong Kong’s role in promoting overseas use of the yuan as well as boosting the city’s financial connectivity with the mainland.

title: TalkTalk Wholesale Arm Is Said to Draw Interest From Telecel
summary: Telecel Group is among bidders competing to acquire TalkTalk Telecom Group’s wholesale telecommunications business, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Panetta Sees Fragile Outlook With Risks to Inflation, Growth
summary: The European Central Bank is navigating an uncertain economic situation even as the US and Iran move closer to a lasting peace deal, according to Governing Council member Fabio Panetta.

title: German Industry Grows More Than Expected on Auto Sector
summary: German industrial production grew for a second month, another sign that Europe’s largest economy is exiting the drag from the Iran war.

title: China Touts Yuan as US Ups Strong Dollar: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wall Street Warms to India’s Markets as Oil Pressures Fade
summary: After spending more than a year on the sidelines, global investors are showing fresh interest in India as sentiment begins to improve across the country’s markets.
